articles.question: How do I grant microphone access?
articles.answer:
Mac

  1. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Microphone
  2. Find Verby in the list and toggle it on
  3. If Verby isn't listed, click the + button and add it from your Applications folder
  4. Restart Verby after granting access

Windows

  1. Open Settings → Privacy → Microphone
  2. Ensure Allow apps to access your microphone is turned on
  3. Scroll down and make sure Verby is toggled on

articles.question: How do I enable accessibility permissions?
articles.answer:
Mac

  1. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility
  2. Click the lock icon to make changes
  3. Find Verby and toggle it on
  4. If prompted, click Restart to apply changes

Windows

Accessibility permissions are granted automatically on Windows. If text injection isn't working, try running Verby as Administrator once.

articles.question: Why does Verby need screen recording permission?
articles.answer:
Verby uses screen recording permission on Mac to detect which app you're currently using. This powers the auto-context feature — Verby adjusts its behavior based on whether you're in an email client, code editor, or chat app.

  1. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security → Screen Recording
  2. Toggle Verby on
  3. Restart Verby after granting access

Verby never records or stores your screen content.

articles.question: The app won't start or gets stuck on loading
articles.answer:
1. Mac: If you see "Verby can't be opened," right-click the app and select Open, then click Open again in the dialog
  2. Windows: If Windows Defender blocks the app, click More info → Run anyway
  3. Make sure you're running the latest version — check the download page for updates
  4. Try quitting Verby completely (check the system tray/menu bar) and restarting
  5. If the issue persists, delete the config folder and restart:
    Mac: ~/Library/Application Support/verbyprompt/
    Windows: %APPDATA%/verbyprompt/

articles.question: My hotkey isn't working or conflicts with another app
articles.answer:
Verby uses these default hotkeys:

  • Alt+Space — Toggle recording
  • Fn key (hold) — Push-to-talk (Mac only)
  • Ctrl+Shift+Space — Toggle dictation mode

If another app uses the same shortcut, that app may capture it first. Common conflicts:

  • Spotlight (Mac) — Uses Cmd+Space by default (no conflict)
  • Alfred/Raycast — May use Alt+Space. Change theirs or Verby's
  • Windows PowerToys — May capture Alt+Space for window management

To fix: close the conflicting app, or change its shortcut. Verby's shortcuts will be customizable in a future update.

articles.question: How can I improve transcription accuracy?
articles.answer:
• Speak clearly at a normal pace — rushing reduces accuracy
  • Use a good microphone — built-in laptop mics work but a headset or USB mic is much better
  • Reduce background noise — close windows, move away from fans/AC
  • Speak in complete phrases rather than single words — the AI model uses context to improve accuracy
  • Check your input device — make sure Verby is using the right microphone in your system sound settings

articles.question: What languages does Verby support?
articles.answer:
Verby supports 50+ languages through its speech-to-text engine. The most accurate results are in:

  • English (US, UK, AU)
  • Spanish
  • French
  • German
  • Japanese
  • Chinese (Mandarin)
  • Portuguese
  • Italian
  • Korean

Other languages are supported but may have lower accuracy. Verby auto-detects your language — no configuration needed.

articles.question: Transcription not appearing or text isn't being injected
articles.answer:
1. Check accessibility permission — text injection requires this on Mac (see Setup & Permissions)
  2. Check the auto-inject setting — open Settings and make sure Auto-inject text is toggled on
  3. Click into a text field first — Verby injects text at the cursor position. Make sure a text input is focused
  4. Try a different app — some apps (like Terminal or certain Electron apps) may not support text injection
  5. Restart Verby — if permissions were recently granted, the app may need a restart

articles.question: How do I control punctuation and formatting?
articles.answer:
Verby automatically adds punctuation based on your speech patterns. Tips:

  • Pause briefly at the end of sentences for automatic periods
  • Say "question mark" or "exclamation point" to insert them
  • Say "new line" or "new paragraph" for line breaks
  • Say "comma" for explicit comma placement

For more control, use Verby's Styles feature to set a tone (professional, casual, etc.) which affects formatting output.

articles.question: What voice commands can I use?
articles.answer:
Verby supports natural voice commands during dictation:

  • "Delete that" — removes the last injected text
  • "New line" / "New paragraph" — inserts line breaks
  • "Period" / "Comma" / "Question mark" — inserts punctuation
  • "Select all" / "Copy" / "Paste" — triggers keyboard shortcuts

Voice commands work in dictation mode (Ctrl+Shift+Space). In prompt mode (Alt+Space), your speech is processed by the AI instead.

articles.question: The overlay isn't showing up
articles.answer:
1. Check if Verby is running — look for the Verby icon in your menu bar (Mac) or system tray (Windows)
  2. Press Alt+Space — this toggles the overlay visibility
  3. Check Show in Dock setting — if disabled, the overlay may be behind other windows. Click the menu bar icon to bring it forward
  4. Restart Verby — quit completely and reopen
  5. Check display settings — on multi-monitor setups, the overlay may appear on a different screen

articles.question: The recording indicator is stuck
articles.answer:
1. Press Alt+Space to stop the current recording
  2. If that doesn't work, press Escape
  3. Check your microphone — if the mic was disconnected during recording, the indicator may freeze
  4. Restart Verby — quit from the menu bar/tray and reopen
  5. If this happens frequently, please report the issue using the help panel so we can investigate

articles.question: Verby is using too much CPU or memory
articles.answer:
• Check your history size — a very large prompt history can slow things down. Go to Settings and clear old history
  • Close and reopen Verby — this resets memory usage
  • Check for updates — newer versions include performance improvements
  • Disable auto-context if you don't need it — it polls the active window periodically

Normal CPU usage should be under 5% when idle. During recording and transcription, brief spikes to 15-30% are normal.

articles.question: Keyboard shortcut not responding
articles.answer:
1. Make sure Verby is running — check the menu bar/system tray
  2. Check for hotkey conflicts — see the "Hotkey conflicts" article under Setup
  3. On Mac: Go to System Settings → Privacy & Security → Accessibility and make sure Verby is listed and enabled
  4. On Windows: Try running Verby as Administrator once
  5. Restart Verby — global shortcuts sometimes need to be re-registered after sleep/wake

articles.question: The app won't update
articles.answer:
1. Check Settings → Updates — click "Check for updates" manually
  2. Download directly — visit the download page to get the latest version
  3. On Mac: If the update fails to install, drag the new .dmg file to Applications manually
  4. On Windows: Close Verby completely (check Task Manager), then run the installer
  5. Clear the update cache:
    Mac: Delete ~/Library/Caches/verbyprompt-updater/
    Windows: Delete %LOCALAPPDATA%/verbyprompt-updater/

articles.question: How do I activate my license key?
articles.answer:
1. Open Verby and go to Settings (gear icon)
  2. Scroll to the Account section
  3. Enter your email address (the one you used to purchase)
  4. Click Activate
  5. Your Pro features will unlock immediately

If activation fails, make sure you're using the exact email from your purchase confirmation. Contact verbysupport@syntrixdev.com if issues persist.

articles.question: How do I check my plan and usage?
articles.answer:
1. Open Verby and go to Settings
  2. Your current plan is shown in the Account section
  3. Daily usage (transcriptions today) is displayed below your plan

Free plan: Limited daily transcriptions
Pro plan: Unlimited transcriptions + advanced features

articles.question: How do I upgrade or change my plan?
articles.answer:
To upgrade to Pro:

  1. Visit verbyai.com/pricing
  2. Choose your plan and complete payment
  3. You'll receive a confirmation email
  4. Activate your license in Verby Settings (see "Activate license key")

To downgrade: your Pro access continues until the end of your billing period. No action needed — it reverts to Free automatically.

articles.question: How do I cancel or get a refund?
articles.answer:
To cancel: Visit your billing portal to manage or cancel your subscription. You can also email verbysupport@syntrixdev.com and we'll handle it for you.

Refund policy: We offer a full refund within 14 days of purchase, no questions asked. Email verbysupport@syntrixdev.com with your purchase email.

articles.question: How do I reset my account?
articles.answer:
To reset your Verby account data:

  1. Open Settings → scroll to the bottom
  2. Click Clear History to remove all saved prompts
  3. Sign out and sign back in to refresh your session

To delete your account entirely, email verbysupport@syntrixdev.com and we'll remove all your data within 48 hours.
